name: Top AI Research Papers 2025
description: The most influential and cited papers in artificial intelligence, large language models, and machine learning published in 2025.
author: trelk-team

items.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Introduces DeepSeek-R1, a model trained with large-scale reinforcement learning to develop reasoning capabilities without supervised fine-tuning, achieving performance comparable to OpenAI-o1 on math and coding benchmarks.

items.title: Titans: Learning to Memorize at Test Time
items.summary: Proposes a new architecture that learns to memorize at test time, combining the efficiency of linear attention with long-term memory modules that can persist information across very long contexts.

items.title: Native Sparse Attention: Hardware-Aligned and Natively Trainable Sparse Attention
items.summary: Presents a sparse attention mechanism that is natively trainable and aligned with modern GPU hardware, enabling efficient processing of sequences up to 64K tokens without quality loss.

items.title: LIMO: Less is More for Reasoning
items.summary: Demonstrates that complex mathematical reasoning can be elicited with surprisingly few examples, achieving state-of-the-art results on competition-level math by fine-tuning on only 817 carefully curated samples.

items.title: SWE-Lancer: Can Frontier LLMs Earn $1 Million from Real-World Freelance Software Engineering?
items.summary: Benchmarks frontier LLMs on real freelance software engineering tasks from Upwork, evaluating whether AI can complete professional coding jobs end-to-end.

items.title: OmniHuman-1: Rethinking the Scaling-Up of One-Stage Conditioned Human Animation Models
items.summary: A unified framework for generating lifelike human animation from any combination of input signals including audio, text, and pose, achieving state-of-the-art results in video generation.

items.title: Multimodal Large Language Models: A Survey
items.summary: Comprehensive survey covering the landscape of multimodal LLMs in 2025, including architectures, training paradigms, evaluation benchmarks, and applications across vision, audio, and video.

items.title: Scaling Laws for Precision in LLM Training and Inference
items.summary: Establishes scaling laws for numerical precision in both training and inference of large language models, providing guidelines for optimal quantization strategies at different model scales.

items.title: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning Without Prompting
items.summary: Shows that chain-of-thought reasoning can emerge naturally in LLMs during decoding without any special prompting, by altering the decoding strategy to explore alternative top-k token paths.

items.title: Distilling System 2 into System 1
items.summary: Proposes methods to distill the slow, deliberate reasoning of System 2 (chain-of-thought) into fast, intuitive System 1 responses, making models both accurate and efficient at inference time.

items.title: Reward Hacking in RLHF: Causes, Detection, and Mitigation
items.summary: Systematically analyzes reward hacking in reinforcement learning from human feedback, identifying failure modes where models exploit reward signals without genuinely improving alignment.

items.title: Constitutional AI 2.0: Democratic Input for AI Behavior
items.summary: Extends constitutional AI with democratic processes for determining AI behavior, incorporating diverse human values through structured deliberation and voting mechanisms.

items.title: KV-Cache Compression for Long-Context LLMs: A Survey
items.summary: Surveys techniques for compressing key-value caches in transformer models to enable efficient long-context processing, covering eviction, quantization, merging, and architectural approaches.

items.title: ToolGen: Unified Tool Retrieval and Calling via Generation
items.summary: Unifies tool retrieval and calling into a single generative framework, enabling LLMs to discover and use tools from large repositories without separate retrieval systems.

items.title: World Models for Autonomous Driving: A Survey
items.summary: Reviews world models applied to autonomous driving, covering how neural networks learn predictive models of driving environments for planning, simulation, and safety verification.

items.title: Mixture of Experts Meets Mixture of Modalities
items.summary: Proposes an architecture that combines mixture-of-experts routing with multimodal inputs, enabling efficient specialization where different experts handle different modality combinations.

items.title: Test-Time Training for LLMs: A New Paradigm
items.summary: Introduces test-time training for language models where the model adapts its weights at inference using the test input itself, achieving significant improvements on distribution-shifted tasks.

items.title: Synthetic Data Generation at Scale: Best Practices and Pitfalls
items.summary: Provides a comprehensive guide to generating synthetic training data for LLMs, covering quality filters, diversity metrics, decontamination, and when synthetic data helps vs hurts.

items.title: Small Language Models: A State-of-the-Art Review
items.summary: Reviews the rapidly advancing field of small language models (1-7B parameters), analyzing distillation techniques, architecture innovations, and deployment strategies for edge devices.

items.title: Agentic AI: A Survey of Foundation Model Agents
items.summary: Comprehensive survey of AI agents built on foundation models, covering planning, tool use, memory, multi-agent collaboration, and real-world deployment challenges.
